title: Rapid Smoky Jamaican Mulukhiyah Stew
description: A quick, smoky Jamaican-inspired mulukhiyah stew that blends tender green leaves with smoked pork, jerk spices, and a fermented ginger garlic sauce for a moist, satisfying single-pot meal.
instructions: Heat a large pot over medium heat and add diced smoked pork, cooking until browned.
Add diced onion and minced garlic, sautéing until onions are translucent.
Stir in smoked paprika, jerk seasoning, and ferment-jang, coating the pork mixture.
Pour in the chicken stock and bring to a gentle boil.
Add the fresh mulukhiyah leaves, stirring to combine.
Reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes, allowing the leaves to wilt and flavors to meld.
Season the stew with salt and black pepper to taste.
Stir occasionally to prevent sticking at the bottom of the pot.
Once the stew is fragrant and the leaves are tender, remove from heat.
Serve hot over steamed rice or with a side of boiled sweet potatoes.
